questions.question: Four words are given in which only one is correctly spelt. Find the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: Buccaneer means pirate, a person who operates in a recklessly adventurous and unscrupulous way. There are three syllables in buccaneer: buc-ca-neer. Correct spellings of other options are: Consign, Divisive and Butcher.

questions.question: Four words are given in which only one is correctly spelt. Find the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: Denouement means outcome, final stage or result. There are three syllables in denouement: de-noue-ment. Correct spellings of other options are: Approbation, Credulity, Fetus.

questions.question: In the following question, a sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active Voice. The cook puts the fish in a pan with some oil.
options: The fish had been put in a pan with some oil by the cook.
The fish has been put in a pan with some oil by the cook.
The fish was put in a pan with some oil by the cook.
The fish is put in a pan with some oil by the cook.
questions.explanation: The sentence is in active voice and in simple present tense (puts). A is incorrect as it is in past perfect tense (had been put). B is incorrect as 'has been put' is in present perfect tense. C is incorrect as 'was put' is in simple past tense. D is the right answer as 'is put' is in simple present tense.

questions.question: very ______ at
questions.explanation: The word we are looking for is 'upset'. Flattered- impressed (exactly opposite). 'Resent' is a verb while we need an adjective. 'Sorely' means upset but is an adverb while we need an adjective. Indignant- aggrieved, unhappy. Hence, A is the correct answer.

questions.question: and _______ a fleet and
questions.explanation: The blank is to be filled with a verb in past tense as per the context. All the four options are synonymous among one another but only 'equipped' satisfies the requirement and hence, C is the correct answer. A and B are in the simple present tense. D is incorrect as it should take the preposition 'by' instead of 'with'.

questions.question: thus ________ across the German
questions.explanation: 'Assembly'- noun, out of context. 'Conveyed'- does not make sense here. 'Muster'- to assemble (fits the context but is not in its correct tense/form). Hence, D is the correct answer.

questions.question: who was, in the _______, assembling his forces,
questions.explanation: 'While' and 'time'- do not make sense. 'In between'- incorrect usage of the phrase. Hence, B is the correct answer.

questions.question: and ________ danger.
questions.explanation: The statement talks about some sudden danger. 'Anticipated' and 'gradual' are exactly opposite of what the writer is trying to convey; an event cannot be sudden and anticipated/gradual at the same time). 'Abruptly'- Adverb which do not qualify the noun 'danger'. Hence, A is the correct answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. Moreover, the presence of citric acid in the drink also maximises the enzyme function. Q. The combination of warm water with lemon juice acts as a natural diuretic. R. It aids in release of toxins from our bodies at a faster rate than usual. S. This maximization keeps our liver healthy.
questions.explanation: P cannot start the passage as P starts with the adverb 'moreover', which shows additionality of information. R and S contain pronouns, so they cannot be the opening statement. Q begins the passage by telling us about the natural property of water with lemon juice. R talks more about this property. P gives an additional property of the same. S concludes by giving the benefit of the property mentioned in P. Option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. As a consequence, it stops inflammation and pain. Q. Due to this alkalising property of the drink, it helps in removing uric acid from our joints. R. Lemons are an incredibly alkaline food. S. While they are acidic independently, once they enter our bodies, they metabolize and become alkaline.
questions.explanation: R starts the passage by introducing the topic of discussion i.e. lemons as incredible alkaline food. 'They' in statement S refers to 'lemons' in R. RS is a pair. SQ is also a pair since Q talks about alkalising property of lemons introduced in S. RSQ is a mandatory set. Option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, a sentence is given in either direct or indirect speech. Select the option that correctly converts the sentence in direct speech to indirect speech or vice versa. "Tomorrow, we will go to war," said the king.
options: The king said that tomorrow we would go to war.
The king said that they will go to war the next day.
The king said that they would go to war the next day.
The king said that we would go to war the next day.
questions.explanation: When converting direct to indirect speech, the simple future tense 'will go' will change to the future conditional 'would go'. 'Tomorrow' will change to 'the next day'. The first person subjective plural pronoun 'we' will change to the third person subjective plural pronoun 'they'. C is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the word similar in meaning to the word given. Opulent
questions.explanation: 'Opulent' means ostentatiously costly and luxurious. 'Sumptuous' means splendid and expensive-looking. Clearly, the two words are synonyms of each other, making option A, the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the word similar in meaning to the word given. Hegemony
questions.explanation: 'Hegemony' means leadership or dominance, especially by one state or social group over others. 'Supremacy' means the state or condition of being superior to all others in authority, power, or status. Clearly, the two words are synonyms of each other, making option B, the correct answer. 'Cowardice' means lack of bravery.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/Phrase. Fall back
questions.explanation: The idiom "Fall back" refers to retreating or receding.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/Phrase. Spill the beans
questions.explanation: The idiom "Spill the beans" refers to disclosing/revealing a secret.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which can best substitute the given phrase. One who can use either hand with ease
questions.explanation: One who is hard to please (very selective in his habits) = Fastidious. One who is recovering health after illness = Convalescent. An unconventional style of living = Bohemian. Hence, option D will be the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which can best substitute the given phrase. A large building with an extensive floor area, typically for housing aircraft
questions.explanation: A place in a large institution for the care of those who are ill = Infirmary. A place where bodies are kept for identification = Morgue. An establishment for the medical treatment of people = Sanatorium. Hence, option B will be the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the word opposite in meaning to the word given. Admonish
questions.explanation: 'Admonish' means to scold firmly. Clearly, the opposite of scold would be 'praise', making option A, the correct answer. 'Pronounce' means to make the sound of (a word or part of a word) in a correct or a particular way.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the word opposite in meaning to the word given. Fallible
questions.explanation: 'Fallible' means capable of making mistakes or being wrong. Clearly, the opposite would be 'flawless' (without any imperfections or defects), making option C, the correct answer.

questions.question: Some parts of the sentences have errors and some are correct. Find out which part of a sentence has an error and choose the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, choose "No Error" as option. My brilliant daughter always do (1)/ something different in every science fair (2)/ that gets her the first prize. (3)/ No error (4)
questions.explanation: The correct sentence is "My brilliant daughter always does something different in every science fair that brings her the first prize". Here, the subject of the given sentence is 'My brilliant daughter', which is singular. So, the verb should also be singular. 'Do' is a plural verb. It should be replaced by its singular form 'does'.

questions.question: Some parts of the sentences have errors and some are correct. Find out which part of a sentence has an error and choose the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, choose "No Error" as option. A series of textbooks are (1)/ available in the market (2) which handles the topic in detail. (3)/ No error (4)
questions.explanation: The correct sentence is "A series of textbooks is available in the market which handles the topic in detail". Here, the subject is 'A series of textbooks'. Textbooks is plural, but 'a series of textbooks' should be treated as singular. Hence, 'are' should be replaced by 'is' which is singular. Similarly 'a bunch of bananas', 'a team of/ a group of/ a gang of physicians', 'a crowd of people', ' a pair of scissors', etc are also treated as singular as they are collective nouns.

questions.question: A sentence is given with a blank to be filled in with appropriate word. Four alternatives are suggested. Choose the correct option. My niece is ______, she always complains about this or that.
questions.explanation: Irritant means something that causes irritation. Pugnacious means eager to argue quarrel or fight. Peevish means always complaining; showing annoyance or irritation. Sheepish means lacking confidence. The appropriate word is 'peevish' meaning dissatisfied or always complaining.

questions.question: A sentence, a part of the sentence is bracketed. Below are given alternatives to the bracketed part which may improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative. In case no improvement is needed choose "No Improvement". I felt refreshed after I (have had) a good night's sleep.
questions.explanation: The past perfect form of have is "had had (had + past participle form of have)". It is used when we are talking about the past and want to refer back to a time in the past.

questions.question: In the question given, a part of the sentence is bracketed. Below are given alternatives to the bracketed part which may improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative. In case no improvement is requires, choose 'No Improvement' option. One of the baby elephants (broke off) from the herd and lost its way.
questions.explanation: Option D is eliminated, as the sentence needs improvement. 'broke off' means to suddenly stop doing something or speaking. Option A is eliminated, as the prepositional phrase 'broke down' means to batter something, while the correct prepositional phrase which must be used here is 'broke away' meaning to get estranged from a group. Option B is the right answer. Option C is eliminated as the prepositional phrase 'broke up' is used to refer to the end of a relationship.

questions.question: A sentence is given with a blank to be filled in with appropriate word. Four alternatives are suggested. Choose the correct option. He was ______ with poetic gifts.
questions.explanation: Dump means to deposit or dispose of in a careless manner. Embed means to fix firmly or deeply. Embark means to go on board a ship or aircraft. Endow means to provide with quality, ability or asset. Poetic gifts cannot be embedded, embarked or dumped. Hence, endowed is the correct word.
